trade.summary: Modest trade relationship. Brasile exports soybeans, coffee, iron ore, and seafood products. Norvegia exports offshore oil equipment, salmon, and maritime technology. The relationship is more significant for environmental cooperation than trade.

diplomatic.summary: Strong diplomatic ties especially around Amazon conservation. Norvegia is the largest single contributor to the Amazon Fund (having contributed over $1 billion). Under Lula, cooperation on Amazon protection has been highlighted as a flagship bilateral achievement.

military.summary: No military alliance. Very limited military cooperation. Norvegia is NATO member; Brasile is non-aligned.

financial.summary: Norvegia's sovereign wealth fund (GPFG) holds Brasileian equities. Equinor has some Brasileian offshore oil interests. Norwegian financial flows to Amazon Fund are the most significant bilateral financial feature.

energy.summary: Equinor (formerly Statoil) has offshore oil operations in Brasile. Norvegia provides technical expertise in offshore oil and subsea technology. Norvegia also funds renewable energy transition projects in Brasile.

scientific.summary: Strong cooperation in environmental science, tropical ecology, and ocean research. Norwegian research institutions partner with Brasileian universities. NORAD funds conservation and research programs in the Amazon.
